rulings.Case Title: MONROE AMEY VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, May 9, 2017 in Department 503, Line 4. Specially appearing defendant Williams & Burrows, Inc.'s motion for order setting aside default judgment is denied. Williams & Burrows did not demonstrate grounds for the Court to invoke its inherent, equitable power to set aside a default judgment on the ground of extrinsic fraud or mistake. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)

rulings.Case Title: LINDA PONCHIONE VS. CONSOLIDATED INSULATION, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, May 9, 2017 in Department 503, Line 6. Defendant Curtis Wiggins Enterprises, Inc.'s motion for judgment on the pleadings of plaintiffs' third and fourth causes of action is denied. Defendant's motion is denied as untimely pursuant to plaintiffs' objection. (C.C.P. § 438(e).) Even if it is timely, the motion is denied because defendant failed to demonstrate that the complaint does not state facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action against it. (C.C.P. § 438(c)(1)(B)(ii).) Defendant's request for judicial notice is granted as to Exhibit 1 and granted in part as to Exhibit 2. The Court will take judicial notice of the existence of the April 7, 2017 order granting CSK Auto, Inc.'s motion for summary adjudication but will not take judicial notice of the truth of facts and findings stated in that order. Finally, even if the Court takes judicial notice of the entirety of Exhibit 2, that ruling does not constitute law of the case. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)
